scope: UniswapX on-chain Reactor settlement surface. Etherscan v2 txlist (chain 1, offset=80, sort=desc) for the high-traffic mainnet reactors: V1 ExclusiveDutchOrderReactor 0x6000da47483062A0D734Ba3dc7576Ce6A0B645C4 and V2DutchOrderReactor 0x00000011F84B9aa48e5f8aA8B9897600006289Be. V3DutchOrderReactor 0x0000000015757c461808EA25Eb309638B62681cf returned 0 direct txs in mainnet txlist (low/zero direct settlement traffic via that address). 160 raw txs (80 each from V1+V2), deduped to 3 distinct (reactor,selector) on-chain callkeys. Permit2 cancel (invalidateUnorderedNonces 0x3ff9dcb1) already covered (uniswapx/cancel-order/cancel@1.0.0) — not re-pulled.
command: set -a; source crates/integration-tests/.env; set +a; for a in 0x6000da47483062A0D734Ba3dc7576Ce6A0B645C4 0x00000011F84B9aa48e5f8aA8B9897600006289Be 0x0000000015757c461808EA25Eb309638B62681cf; do curl -s "https://api.etherscan.io/v2/api?chainid=1&module=account&action=txlist&address=$a&startblock=0&endblock=99999999&page=1&offset=80&sort=desc&apikey=$ETHERSCAN_API_KEY" -o es_$a.json; done; (build 3-entry deduped corpus -> crates/integration-tests/data/golden/v3-decode/uniswapx/corpus.json); ./target/debug/v3-harness corpus

coverage_matrix.fix: hand-corpus/SDK fixture needed for positive-decode test (no Etherscan source for off-chain sigs)

coverage_soft.name: execute((bytes,bytes))
coverage_soft.detail: DOMINANT real-world settlement path (V1 57/80, V2 80/80). No manifest registered for reactor addresses, so callkey {chain}__{reactor}__0x3f62192e has no mapper. Needs a reactor manifest; SignedOrder=(bytes order, bytes sig) inner decode is Tier B.

coverage_soft.name: executeBatch((bytes,bytes)[])
coverage_soft.detail: Batch settle of SignedOrder[] (V1 23/80). array_emit + Tier B per-element SignedOrder decode.

coverage_soft.name: executeWithCallback((bytes,bytes),bytes)
coverage_soft.detail: Filler-callback single settle. Not in last-80 sample but a valid reactor entrypoint. Same Tier B SignedOrder decode.

coverage_soft.name: executeBatchWithCallback((bytes,bytes)[],bytes)
coverage_soft.detail: Filler-callback batch settle. array_emit + Tier B per-element.

summary: UniswapX on-chain Reactor settlement is entirely uncovered. The real high-traffic surface is the filler calling execute / executeBatch against the canonical reactors (mainnet V1 ExclusiveDutchOrderReactor 0x6000da47, V2DutchOrderReactor 0x00000011F8), NOT the Permit2 nonce cancel that is the only on-chain UniswapX manifest today. Last-80 sample: V2 reactor is 80/80 execute (0x3f62192e); V1 is execute 57/80 + executeBatch 23/80 (0x0d7a16c3). The V3 reactor address (0x0000000015) showed 0 direct mainnet txs. All settlement txs route to soft/no_declarative_v3_mapper — a pure coverage gap (no reactor address appears in any callkey). Adding manifests for reactor addresses is the quick win, but the bundled SignedOrder=(bytes order, bytes sig) cannot be decoded declaratively: single_emit only reaches the outer (bytes,bytes) tuple; the inner `order` bytes is a separately ABI-encoded per-reactor struct (DutchOrder / V2DutchOrder / etc.) => Tier B decoder is the real blocker (executeBatch needs array_emit + Tier B per element). The 4 off-chain order types (ExclusiveDutch/V2Dutch/V3Dutch/Priority) already have typed-data manifests (sentinel selectors 0x00000001-04) but no positive-decode corpus because Etherscan has no off-chain signatures — they need SDK/hand fixtures. Reactor addresses + selectors verified against 1st-party Uniswap sources (uniswapx-sdk/src/constants.ts REACTOR_ADDRESS_MAPPING, developers.uniswap.org/contracts/uniswapx/deployments) and local keccak256 (selftest: transfer(address,uint256)=0xa9059cbb).

baseline.note: no manifests / index / decoder modified by this diagnosis — corpus + log only. The 6 layerzero MISSes in the full corpus run are a sibling agent's in-progress work, outside this task.
